---
title: "Platform"
subtitle: "Infrastructure build, operations, and runbooks for the UIAO governance substrate"
doc-type: section-landing
audience: "Platform engineers · sysadmins · integrators"
canon-source: "src/uiao/canon/adr/adr-041-uiao-git-infrastructure.md + src/uiao/canon/release-manifests/platform-server-v1.2.yaml"
derived-from: "ADR-041 (accepted 2026-05-12) + UIAO_SBG_001 build guide"
aspirational: true
---

# Platform {.unnumbered}

Operational runbooks for building, hardening, and governing the **on-premises
UIAO substrate**. These documents describe how to stand up the concrete
infrastructure the canon governs — Gitea behind IIS on Windows Server 2025,
Intune compliance, Azure Arc enrollment, and OrgTree integration — so the
policies in [Modernization Specs](../modernization-specs/index.html) have a
real substrate to attach to.

::: {.callout-note title="Scope"}
Platform pages are **infrastructure runbooks**, not identity architecture.
Identity governance (OrgPath, dynamic groups, AUs, joiner/mover/leaver)
lives in the [Modernization canon](../../modernization/orgtree.html) and
its appendices UIAO_151–UIAO_176. Platform pages *cite* those canonical sources
and show only the server-scoped instantiation.
:::

## Pages in this section

Status badges follow the [Whitepapers status conventions](../whitepapers/index.qmd).

| Slug | Purpose | Status | Canonical source |
|---|---|---|---|
| [`platform-server-build`](./platform-server-build.qmd) | Windows Server 2025 + IIS + Gitea + Intune + Azure Arc + OrgTree — 14-phase build guide (Option B, ADR-041 canon) | Aspirational — operationally authoritative; field-rehearsal pending | `UIAO_SBG_001` (this page) + ADR-041 |
| [`git-server-implementation`](./git-server-implementation.qmd) | Option A (IIS + `git-http-backend.exe` CGI) — lab/POC reference and the source of the canonical Git-hook bodies | Superseded for production — do not deploy without a new ADR | ADR-041 §Alternatives |

## Canonical invariants

Every platform page respects:

- **Boundary:** `gcc-moderate` (M365 SaaS). IaaS components (Azure Arc, Log
  Analytics, Azure Blob backup) sit in commercial FedRAMP per the ADR-041
  commercial-FedRAMP exception.
- **Architecture decision:** Gitea behind IIS reverse proxy on a single
  Windows Server 2025 host (ADR-041, Option B — `status: accepted`
  2026-05-12). Option A (IIS + `git-http-backend.exe` alone) is retained
  for lab/POC use only and is non-canonical for production.
- **OrgTree integration:** every platform server carries an OrgPath, lives
  inside an Administrative Unit, and reports drift state to UIAO_163.
